title: asperella duthiei subsp. longe-aristata
intro: Asperella duthiei subsp. longe-aristata is a type of plant species, belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae, commonly known as the adder's tongue ferns, specifically a variant of the Asperella duthiei species. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Asperella duthiei subsp. longe-aristata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin irritations. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and stems, and then using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
